string.text: The PDA of a stalker nicknamed Trus.
The PDA of a stalker nicknamed Balbes.
The PDA of a stalker nicknamed Byvaly.
Fedor's flash drive. The data is encrypted.
A package with supplies for Maximilian.
The PDA of a dead mercenary.

Last entry:\n Mutny, that bastard, won’t leave me alone now. The deal was profitable, sure, but... witnesses don’t live long. If I die, this log should explain everything.\n Mutny robs stalkers near the first bridge from his base when they return with valuable artifacts from the valley center. I found out by accident and told no one. I decided to blackmail him like a rat. Mutny gave me a small cut of the loot. And the flash drive with the info — I left it with Vasya the Postman. If I die, he’s supposed to decode it and give it to Maximilian. And that’s how it all went down.
Key to the prison cell at the mercenary base.
A microchip for a spatial anomaly scanner.
Scanner tuned to detect spatial anomalies.
Scanner tuned to detect spatial anomalies.
Maximilian’s personal stash. Locked with a key.
Semyon Cowboy’s personal stash. Locked with a key.
Key to Semyon Cowboy’s personal stash.
Key to Ilya Barbos’s personal stash.
Key to a military equipment crate.
Documentation on tuning the spatial anomaly scanner.
Trader’s laptop. Access is restricted; a password is required.

One of the notes:\n The boss gave us a sweet gig. My buddy and I mixed in with some stalkers and spread a rumor about a monster in the abandoned camp. Then we lure greedy fools there one by one. Take their loot, and stash the corpses in the nearby cave...
Flash drive containing personal data and letters from a stalker nicknamed Vadik the Pilot.
Flash drive containing personal data and letters from a stalker nicknamed Romka the Bachelor.
Flash drive with data on anomalous field readings.
Crate containing goods for a trader.
An artifact with an unusual appearance and unknown origin. It possesses a unique property — it can almost completely absorb anomalous psi influence on the wearer.

This artifact actively attracts and absorbs radioactive particles, significantly reducing the radiation exposure of the body. Unofficially used within and beyond the Zone for treating acute radiation sickness.
This artifact accelerates tissue and organ regeneration in living beings and stabilizes metabolic processes. However, it is radioactive.

Last entry:\n Damn it! Looks like I’ll die here. Mutants chased me into this damn cave. Got caught in an anomaly like a clueless tourist...\n And if you’re reading this — I’m already dead. Please, take this PDA to the trader at Maximilian's base. There’s info for him on it. And your reward (not like I’ll need it) — I hid all my stash in a tree near this cursed cave... Ha, someone’s gonna get lucky...
Homemade explosive. Several TNT bricks wired to an electronic detonator.

I got closer to the center... Not much left in terms of artifacts. I didn’t go further.\n Damn, Valery Palych is going to kill me when he finds out I ditched his gun. But what could I do? Beasts attacked — I barely got away. I’ll come back for it later.

Note:
 I finally found the stalker Professor Stakhovsky mentioned. Or rather, what was left of him. Copied the data from his PDA. Must remember to deliver it to the professor.

 Last entry:
 Damn it, why the hell did I come here? And that anomaly — no way out. I get closer to the teleport and it appears somewhere else. It’s like the thing can see what I see. I should just close my eyes. Playing cat-and-mouse with me... Got hit by a zombie, no medkits left... Looks like this is the end...
Last note:\n For those who can’t find me to retrieve the stash key on their own.\n I headed south... Look for me there...
Documentation on tuning the spatial anomaly scanner.
